entities.name: Vrijen
entities.context: Founder of Careerspan, based in downtown Brooklyn. Mangalorean/Gujarati heritage, born in Ahmedabad, raised partly in Bangladesh, college in the US, spent much of his twenties in Singapore.

entities.name: Mihir
entities.context: Founder/team lead of a New York-based company, first-generation Indian-American, born in California, moved to NYC three years ago. Now engaged.

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; product origin and strategy shift mentioned but details truncated.

entities.name: Grand Central Tech
entities.context: NYC-based residency program where Mihir's team previously had free office space near Grand Central.

entities.name: Vrijen's co-founder
entities.context: Vrijen's co-founder at Careerspan, still based in Boston. Met Vrijen in Singapore.

entities.name: cultural honor vs pragmatic business
entities.context: Tension discussed by Vrijen and Mihir as Asian founders navigating cultural expectations against business pragmatism.

entities.name: Asian founder identity
entities.context: Shared identity and cultural connection point between Vrijen and Mihir as Asian founders in tech.

relationships.context: Connected over shared identity as Asian founders; discussed cultural tensions and business strategy.
Vrijen is the founder of Careerspan.
Mihir's team previously used Grand Central Tech's free residency space before outgrowing it.
Co-founders of Careerspan; met in Singapore, co-founder now in Boston.
Vrijen identifies and discusses the tension between cultural honor and pragmatic business decisions as an Asian founder.
Mihir connects with Vrijen over this shared tension as a first-generation Indian-American founder.
Mangalorean/Gujarati heritage, multicultural upbringing across India, Bangladesh, US, and Singapore.
First-generation Indian-American, born in California, now based in New York.
